[185:57]Catholicism is the truth. We are not advocating for religion in general.

[186:02]We're not advocating for a belief in in one God in general.

[186:07]We're advocating for a belief in a particular God,

[186:11]a particular religion, a particular church.

[186:20]We're not saying Allah is God.

[186:23]We're not saying Yahweh is God.

[186:25]We're not saying Vishnu is one of the gods.

[186:29]We're saying Jesus Christ is God.

[186:31]The Catholic Church is the church that he founded.

[186:36]We're saying that since America is a Christian nation,

[186:41]since America was founded by Christians, settled by Christians,

[186:46]up until very recently was occupied mostly by Christians,

[186:50]since its constitution and its culture are derived from Christendom

[186:55]and derived from the Catholic Church,

[186:58]our governance should reflect that, our society should reflect that.

[187:02]There are Muslim countries.

[187:05]There is a Jewish country and there are other kinds of countries, they're communist countries.

[187:17]Christian country for that to happen.

[187:20]We want Christians making the laws.

[187:22]We want Christians interpreting the laws.

[187:24]We want Christians enforcing the laws.

[187:31]in our schools, in our media.

[187:33]We want Christian morals to inform

[187:36]the content of the laws themselves.

[187:42]This is actually contrary to the Enlightenment.

[187:46]This is contrary to the revolutions

[187:49]of the 17th, 18th, 19th, and 20th centuries.

[187:54]This is a counter-revolution.

[187:57]This is reaction.

[188:03]like pluralism and like tolerance, individualism,

[188:09]blank slate view of human nature.

[188:18]And what's interesting about that phrase is unlike one nation under God, not everybody

[188:24]can and not everybody will say it.

[188:28]And it's interesting.

[188:30]When you go to some kind of conservative, Republican, liberal, democratic event, and

[188:38]they say one nation under God, everybody can put their hand over their heart and say that

[188:44]when they say, may God protect our troops, everybody can say that. But if you go to a

[188:49]political rally and everyone's chanting Christ is king, if you look around the room, you

[188:55]may notice not everybody is saying the chant. Not everybody can. And by nature, it is exclusionary

[189:03]because unlike a vague appeal to God, not everybody can say it. That's because not everybody

[189:11]And saying it is a way to quickly identify who's really with us,

[189:15]who actually has a faith that is strong and alive.

[189:20]Not every Christian wakes up every day and says,

[189:22]Christ is king.

[189:24]Some liberal Christians wake up and say,

[189:26]you know, we should tolerate everybody and everything's fine.

[189:30]Even some Catholics say that.

[189:32]But for a Catholic to wake up every day and say, Christ is king.

[189:35]Christ is your king. He's my king. He's the king. It's Christ himself. It is not just some vague God. It's a particular one

[189:42]That's a very quick way to identify who's with us

[189:53]And Christ is king is something that actually Jewish people cannot participate in

[189:58]And if we're to say that the country should be run by Christians,

[190:02]that's also something that Jews actually can't participate in.

[190:06]And by the way, when we say Christ is king, it highlights

[190:11]the origins of Christianity, which is this.

[190:15]Jesus Christ, who is the center of the Christian religion,

[190:19]arrived saying,

[190:21]I was sent by God.

[190:22]I'm the son.

[190:23]This is what Jesus said.

[190:24]I'm the son of God.

[190:25]I am the king.

[190:27]The Jews didn't agree.

[190:29]Some of them did, but not all of them did.

[190:32]Some of the Jews agreed, the disciples and the early church, they agreed.

[190:39]The Jewish authorities that lived in the Holy Land at the time did not.

[190:49]Jesus because he said that.

[190:55]humiliated because what they were expecting was a real king. They were

[191:00]expecting what was conventionally thought of as a king or historically was the

[191:06]king of the Jews. They were expecting a king like David. They were expecting a

[191:12]powerful political and military leader who would throw off the Roman Empire and

[191:17]make Israel a powerful nation.

[191:19]And what they got was somebody preaching

[191:22]an eternal spiritual kingdom.

[191:25]He said, my kingdom is not of this world.

[191:27]They didn't like that.

[191:29]They got somebody saying,

[191:31]the commandment is to love everyone like you love yourselves.

[191:35]The person is meant to serve.
